title: Trace determination of nickel-, cadmium-, copper- and zinc(II) using rhodanine-an amperometric reagent
author: Sandhya Gandhe
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, M.L.C. Government Girls P. G. College, Khandwa-450 001, India 

  E-mail :  sandhyagandhe@rediffmail.com 

 Department of Chemistry, Government Auto. M.V. Mahavidyalaya, Bhopal-462 008, India 

 Department ofChemistry, Government Holkar Science Auto. College, Indore-452 017, India 

  Manuscript received 20 March 2002. revised 13 September 2002, accepted 29 October 2002  

 Rhodanine is employed as amperometric reagent for the trace determination of metals such as Ni II , Cd II  , Cu II  and Zn II  in BR buffer at pHs 6.0, 5.5, 4.91 and 4.6, respectively, with  µ  = 0.1  M  (KCl) and fresh 0.001% gelatin. Titration potential has been selected in the limiting region of the cathodic wave of titled metals. The statistical analysis of the observed data reveals high accuracy and precision.
